scenes.title: The Last Ordinary Morning — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Cassian Reed wakes to an ordinary Tuesday in Ashford—until the sky fractures over the harbor and the world ends in a single afternoon.
scenes.caption: Chapter opening — Ashford before the fracture

scenes.title: The Fractured Harbor
scenes.description: The sky tears above Ashford Harbor as the Rapture begins.
scenes.subtitle: Ashford, Day Zero
scenes.caption: The sky tears above Ashford Harbor as the Rapture begins.

scenes.title: The Sound Above — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Fire captain Rowan Hale moves his family toward safety when the sky tears open over Ashford's outskirts—and something vast emerges from the wound.

scenes.title: Falling Fire — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Adrian and Selene face two days of fracturing sky from their apartment above the bakery—until the sky breaks fully and the first Ember Dragon descends on Ashford.

scenes.title: When The World Ended — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Three survivors on Day Three: Cassian navigates a city turning predatory, Rowan calculates who can be saved at the barn, and Adrian searches for passage south while Ashford burns from every direction.

scenes.title: Day Zero — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Marcus Vale attempts to evacuate with Iris and six-year-old Lily through Ashford's university district—but the organized convoy fails, the roads break, and Marcus finds himself choosing documentation over denial as civilization collapses around his family.

scenes.title: The Photographer — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Wildlife photographer Nora Winters witnesses the Rapture from open country, moves against the flow of refugees into the wilderness, and encounters a displaced silver hawk whose trust she earns—while her camera captures something in the fracture light that belongs to no field guide.

scenes.title: The First Night — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: On the sixth night of the Rapture, Cassian Reed barricades his apartment, rations what remains, and learns what the city sounds like after dark—until something scrapes at his door and he holds his breath until dawn.

scenes.title: The Things In The Dark — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Cassian's first direct encounter with a grave-walker turns into a messy flight through Ashford's dead streets — and leaves him shaken by something impossible happening in his own hands.

scenes.title: No Rescue Is Coming — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Eight days after the sky broke, Rowan Hale cycles through every emergency frequency he knows and finds only silence — learning, finally, that no one is coming.
scenes.caption: Rowan at the rescue barn — Ashford outskirts, Day 8

scenes.title: The Last Broadcast — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: On the ninth day after the Rapture, a final emergency broadcast reaches five survivors in five different places — and ends with a sentence that closes the door on the world they knew.

scenes.title: Empty Shelves — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Cassian scavenges Ashford's depleted shops on the tenth day and meets the first real test of who he intends to be when kindness carries a cost.

scenes.title: The Hale Hold — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Rowan formalizes the rescue barn's shelter rules and loses someone he promised to protect — learning the difference between a promise kept in intention and one the world refuses to honor.

scenes.title: Broken Chain — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Adrian searches for Selene and Rowan through the ruins of Ashford's eastern margin, pulling stragglers along as he goes — until a piece of cloth on the wrong side of a collapsed overpass stops him cold.

scenes.title: Field Notes — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: In the corner of a refugee gymnasium, Marcus Vale makes the first stable mana readings of the post-Rapture world — while his daughter asks the questions he is only beginning to find language for.

scenes.title: Northbound — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Nora Winters moves north against the refugee tide, nearly drowns at a river crossing, and chooses the wild over the crowd — until three smoke columns on the horizon breathe in the same rhythm.
scenes.caption: Day fifteen — the Central Wilds, moving against the tide

scenes.title: Waiting Room — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Selene Arkwright organizes intake at an eastern refugee camp and waits for Adrian — watching reunion after reunion that is not hers, and learning what fear of abandonment feels like when the world has stopped arguing against it.

scenes.title: Under The Same Sky — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: The same collapsed highway interchange serves as landmark, shelter, and waypoint for four survivors who pass through it within hours of each other — and never meet.

scenes.title: The Healer's Hands — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Elena Hale pushes past exhaustion to save a child she cannot save — and discovers, in the aftermath, that her hands have begun to do something impossible.

scenes.title: Names On A Wall — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Rowan lists the dead of the barn's first week and holds the community together through an argument about burial, memory, and whether grief is a luxury the living can still afford.

scenes.title: Separate Roads — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: The first week ends as it was always going to end — with people moving in different directions for reasons that all make sense and none of which keep anyone together.

scenes.title: Ruined Highways — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Nora Winters maps the changed highways east of Ashford and finds that geography itself has shifted—a city name that was never on any map, and a hawk who will not fly toward the eastern glow.
scenes.caption: Nora maps the broken road while Whisper circles above.

scenes.title: The Changed Road
scenes.description: Nora marks the shifted geography on her map as Whisper watches the eastern horizon.
scenes.subtitle: Central Wilds, Day 21
scenes.caption: Nora marks the shifted geography on her map as Whisper watches the eastern horizon.

scenes.title: Still Searching — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Adrian Hale moves east through the ruin belt, accumulating survivors he cannot abandon while searching for Selene and Rowan—until a child points toward something monstrous and new on the horizon.
scenes.caption: Adrian moves through the ruin belt with a group of survivors who chose to follow him.

scenes.title: The Ruin Belt
scenes.description: Adrian reads the block's monster signature before leading the group through.
scenes.subtitle: Ashford Margins, Day 22
scenes.caption: Adrian reads the block's monster signature before leading the group through.

scenes.title: High Ground — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Cassian Reed scouts defensible positions across Ashford's elevated district—mapping water, walls, and sightlines with the reluctant precision of someone who sees a future city where only ruins stand.
scenes.caption: Cassian maps the ruins of Ashford from the elevated district, marking what could still be saved.

scenes.title: The Meridian View
scenes.description: Cassian's scrap map: three circles, two squares, one annotated X, and the question he would not write down.
scenes.subtitle: Ashford Elevated District, Day 23
scenes.caption: Cassian's scrap map: three circles, two squares, one annotated X, and the question he would not write down.

scenes.title: Questions Without Answers — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Marcus Vale answers his daughter's impossible questions about the broken sky and learns that 'I don't know' is not defeat—until Lily finds a stone warm to the touch, carved with symbols from no language he recognizes.
scenes.caption: Marcus Vale at the campfire with Iris and Lily, the field notebooks spread across his knees.

scenes.title: Field Notes, Day 24
scenes.description: Marcus Vale's notebook: measurements, Lily's questions in the margin, and the sketch of the stone.
scenes.subtitle: Eastern Wilds, Day 24
scenes.caption: Marcus Vale's notebook: measurements, Lily's questions in the margin, and the sketch of the stone.

scenes.title: Watch Rotation — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Rowan Hale formalizes the first guard rotation at the relocated shelter—assigning positions, training civilians who have never held a weapon, watching Atlas absorb every detail, and accepting the title of leader without ceremony.
scenes.caption: Rowan maps the shelter perimeter by hand, Atlas and Leon standing just behind him.

scenes.title: First Watch
scenes.description: Rowan demonstrates the southeast position as Atlas and Owen observe.
scenes.subtitle: Outskirts Shelter, Day 25
scenes.caption: Rowan demonstrates the southeast position as Atlas and Owen observe.

scenes.title: Last Signal — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Selene Arkwright finds the last dead phone tower and discovers her unsent text to Adrian. A false rumor about him cuts deeper than it should—and an omega in Ashford who makes things vanish lodges in her mind like a splinter.
scenes.caption: Selene stands beneath the dead phone tower with her phone in her hand, the screen dark.

scenes.title: The Unsent Message
scenes.description: Selene at the dead tower. In her pocket: a phone with no signal and a draft from Day Seven.
scenes.subtitle: Eastern Refugee Trail, Day 26
scenes.caption: Selene at the dead tower. In her pocket: a phone with no signal and a draft from Day Seven.

scenes.title: Passing Shadows — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Cassian Reed finally leaves Ashford—passing a survivor camp without joining, finding trail markers he can't account for, and choosing loneliness over bad company for the first time as an act of will rather than circumstance.
scenes.caption: Cassian at the edge of Ashford, facing the open road, the city at his back.

scenes.title: The Ashford Edge
scenes.description: Cassian crosses the last of the familiar streets and finds the beginning of the open road.
scenes.subtitle: Southern Ashford Outskirts, Day 27
scenes.caption: Cassian crosses the last of the familiar streets and finds the beginning of the open road.

scenes.title: Impossible Ruins — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: Nora Winters discovers ruins of impossible architecture at the wild zone's edge—wrong angles, pre-human stonework, and stars visible through a gap that should not show stars at midday. Then the ground trembles, and the ruin moves.
scenes.caption: Nora stands at the edge of the ruins as Whisper circles at altitude, refusing to descend.

scenes.title: Wrong Angles
scenes.description: The ruin's interior: symbols and impossible geometry recorded in Nora's field sketches.
scenes.subtitle: Wild Zone Edge, Day 28
scenes.caption: The ruin's interior: symbols and impossible geometry recorded in Nora's field sketches.

scenes.title: The Tremor — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: A single tremor crosses the entire region simultaneously. Three separate groups—Marcus in his mobile lab, Rowan at his shelter, Adrian on the road—feel it from their separate distances. Then the sky clears for sixty seconds and every survivor sees the same impossible shape on the horizon.
scenes.caption: The eastern horizon as seen from three separate distances: the same shape, the same impossible scale.

scenes.title: Beyond The Ruins — Chapter Opening
scenes.description: The first dungeon rises from the earth east of Ashford—a colossal structure visible from every position across the region. Each survivor witnesses it from their own distance, their own isolation. Cassian stands alone on the open road, the last to see it, with no one to answer his question.
scenes.caption: The first dungeon rises at dusk—a colossal structure of stone and amber light visible across the whole eastern horizon.
